codeReview.summary: The feature correctly persists anti-leakage-safe team rolling offense metrics and Marcel blending, but the lineup-weighted path does not satisfy the contract: it is computed from a season-level batting leaderboard snapshot instead of 7/14/30/60 as-of rolling history, which can leak future performance into historical feature rows.

issues.description: _build_lineup_metric_lookup() builds lineup values from fetch_batting_stats(season, min_pa=0) once per date and reuses that same season-level snapshot for every 7/14/30/60 window (compute_offensive_features() then applies it at lines 140-157). fetch_batting_stats() itself has no as-of date parameter (src/clients/statcast_client.py:155-165), so an April backfill can consume later-season player performance, and once the regression blend turns off the 30g/60g lineup features collapse to the same non-rolling value. That violates the feature contract's rolling-window and anti-leakage requirements for lineup-weighted offense.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Windows worker startup guidance is still incomplete. The repo documents Windows-specific install/test commands, but there is no corresponding init command in services.yaml, and this worker again had to deviate from the normal init flow after running bash .factory/init.sh on Windows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback in C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T17-27-04-331Z__t9-offensive-features__1259c3dc-1e2f-4994-bc31-f65be4d6d297.json documents the init deviation and suggests adding an init_windows command; compare C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\services.yaml, which has Windows entries for install, test, typecheck, and lint but no init_windows command, and C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md, which documents the Windows shell gotcha.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state does not currently warn feature workers that fetch_batting_stats() returns season-level FanGraphs leaderboards with no date scoping, making it unsafe for historical anti-leakage lineup feature backfills unless player-level as-of snapshots are persisted elsewhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\src\clients\statcast_client.py:155-165 exposes only season, min_pa, and refresh for batting stats, while C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\architecture.md and C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md describe anti-leakage requirements generally but do not capture this ingestion limitation. The worker handoff's discovered issue also calls out the lack of dated player offensive snapshots.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, shared-state docs, and commit 56de0940c97158784ae8af8d40e989a5ddea68be. Team rolling features and Marcel blending look aligned with the contract, but the lineup-weighted features are sourced from a season-level batting leaderboard snapshot rather than as-of rolling windows, so the feature does not fully satisfy t9-offensive-features or VAL-CROSS-002.
